generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "A LiDAR unit on the vehicle that captures raw point cloud data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "A sensor array that reports vehicle state such as speed, throttle, and steering angle."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorSteeringTelemetrySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Telemetry sensor that streams motor torque and steering wheel position to the twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorSteeringTelemetryDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorSteeringTelemetryDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorSteeringTelemetrySensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized LiDAR point cloud data received via ROS2 over UDP on Wi‑Fi."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state vector including speed, acceleration, and deviation from lane center."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudACCEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorSteeringTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Telemetry data for motor torque and steering wheel position."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DeviceControlEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorSteeringTelemetryDataTransmitted
]

instance ACCModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control model that processes vehicle state to compute desired speed."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudACCEnabler
]

instance SimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model encapsulating the physical state of the vehicle for visualization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCollectionEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processes raw LiDAR point cloud to detect object tracks and project driving paths."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance CloudACCEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Sends vehicle state data to the cloud ACC module and receives control commands."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DeviceControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Transmits control commands from the cloud to vehicle actuators and receives telemetry."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataCollectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Collects processed simulation data for the Data Monitoring service."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "UI service that displays real‑time vehicle state, projected trajectory, and object tracks; allows users to view, save, and generate reports."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateInsight, TrajectoryInsight, ObjectTrackInsight, ViewReportAction, GenerateReportAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Cloud‑based ACC module that receives raw sensor telemetry, computes desired speed, and sends control command back to the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCDesiredSpeedInsight, AdjustAccParamsAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "UI service that enables operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed manually, and issue emergency braking commands over a low‑end‑to‑end‑latency link."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableDisableACCAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakingAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time vehicle state insight derived from vehicle telemetry."
]

instance TrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected driving path insight computed from LiDAR point cloud clustering."
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Object tracking insight showing detected vehicles and obstacles."
]

instance ACCDesiredSpeedInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Desired speed insight computed by the ACC model."
]

instance ViewReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to view historical data reports in the UI."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance GenerateReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to generate a new data report from collected telemetry."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AdjustAccParamsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to adjust ACC parameters and algorithmic weights remotely."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EnableDisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to enable or disable the Adaptive Cruise Control system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to manually set the desired speed for the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Urgent action to command the vehicle to perform emergency braking."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C17: Environment
instance VehicleTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiDARSensor, VehicleStateSensor, MotorSteeringTelemetrySensor,
               LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted, VehicleStateDataTransmitted, MotorSteeringTelemetryDataTransmitted,
               LiDARPointCloudData, VehicleStateData, MotorSteeringTelemetryData,
               PointCloudProcessingEnabler, CloudACCEnabler, DeviceControlEnabler, DataCollectionEnabler,
               ACCModel, SimulationModel,
               DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands generated by the digital twin (e.g., desired speed or emergency braking) are encapsulated as UDP packets via the Wi‑Fi network and delivered to the vehicle’s actuation controllers. Commands are processed by the Data Translation component which formats the message, performs unit‑conversions, and aligns message types. Vehicle actuation hardware executes the commands, and acknowledgment messages reporting execution status are sent back to the digital twin, ensuring round‑trip confirmation. Conflict resolution between concurrent commands is handled by the control‑panel FE, which prioritises safety‑critical instructions."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System requires all component clocks to be synchronized with an accuracy of 100 ms. Network latency from the vehicle to the Digital Twin Entity must not exceed 100 ms. This 100 ms bound is specifically chosen due to hardware limitations, although stricter synchronization (<100 ms) is normally mandated for safety‑critical vehicular applications."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows ISO 23247 Reference Architecture. Each Functional Element (FE) is implemented as an individual ROS 2 package, which may contain one or more ROS 2 nodes. This mapping aligns the software components with the ISO 23247 entity-based structure, resulting in three distinct entities: User, Digital Twin, and Physical Twin. The User Entity hosts the User Interface FE split into a 3D Visualization FE and a Control Panel FE. The Digital Twin Entity houses data storage, preprocessing, and control logic. The Physical Twin Entity interfaces directly with the vehicle’s compute, sensors, and actuators and executes compute‑heavy functions such as point‑cloud clustering and tracking."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Software components communicate over a ROS2 network, while inter‑entity communication occurs over a Wi‑Fi network using UDP as the transport layer. The Data Translation component (ISO 23247 Data Translation FE) manages serialization, message type alignment, and unit conversions across the network."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing, particularly Part 2 (reference architecture). The standard describes an entity‑based architecture comprising an Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), a Data Collection and Device Control Entity, a Digital Twin Entity, and a User Entity, each composed of Functional Entities (FEs) that perform tasks such as actuating the OME, transforming data, and interfacing with users. While ISO 23247 does not prescribe implementation details, it recommends interoperability protocols such as OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) for communication between functional entities."
]
